commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And the king made of the almug wood pillars for the house of the LORD, and for the king's house, harps also and psalteries for singers: there came no such almug wood, nor was seen such unto this day. Hebrew mihsad, "pillars, supporters, or banisters. "(Haydock) Most interpreters suppose the rails were on each side of the road, leading from the palace to the temple. (Calmet) Paralipomenon stairs. Citterns, or harps and lyres. (Haydock) Hebrew, "kinnoroth and nebalim.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
